description: This figure displays Vickers hardness evolution and corresponding contour plots for three Fe-Mn-Al-Cu low-density steels (15Al, 17Al, 19Al) subjected to aging treatments at 525–650 °C for 0 to 10 h. Subplots a, b, and c correspond to 15Al, 17Al, and 19Al alloys respectively: each left line plot presents hardness as a function of aging time at six fixed aging temperatures, while each right contour plot maps hardness distribution across the full tested aging temperature-time space. All alloys exhibit hardness increase from ~250 HV (solution-treated state) to peak values with aging: peak hardness decreases as Al content rises, reaching ~966 HV for 15Al, ~925 HV for 17Al, and ~918 HV for 19Al. The optimal aging temperature window for peak hardness shifts to higher values with increasing Al content, matching the trend of suppressed β-Mn precipitation kinetics at higher Al levels. This figure confirms that Al content and aging parameters can be adjusted to obtain wide-range tunable hardness in the alloy system.
